logs.name: mad-width
logs.text:
>What? Why?

Because simply holding a Nazi flag and saying "I am a Nazi" is not justification in itself for being assaulted

>So it's completely made up?

No, I said "our society should not leave open such a gaping blind spot for violent people to exploit."

>It actually happened

Thousands of people died on 9/11. That doesn't mean I can punch someone in the face for saying "Al Qaeda is awesome"

>Nazis *promise* this.

Ok again, if someone is threatening *imminent, direct violence*,

logs.name: ordered-marlin
logs.text:
> Because simply holding a Nazi flag and saying "I am a Nazi" is not justification in itself for being assaulted

That's not what I'm challenging here. You claimed that

>The term "Nazi" can vary in definition depending on the person you ask, and after our society agrees that it is okay to physically assault Nazis, the definition of the word "Nazi" can be watered down while the connotation of the original definition remains.

I'm asking if someone waving Nazi flags calling themself a Nazi,

logs.name: ordered-marlin
logs.text: @mad-width You're all talk, no action. If you really believed in your cause, you'd be out there punching Nazis yourself instead of hiding behind your keyboard. And who are you to say what's okay for society? You're just another armchair activist.

logs.name: mad-width
logs.text: @ordered-marlin, I'm not hiding behind my keyboard. I'm having a discussion about principles and the rule of law. I believe in standing up for what's right, but that doesn't mean I advocate for vigilante justice. If you think punching people is the solution, you're part of the problem. I'm not an armchair activist; I'm a responsible citizen who believes in due process and the law.

logs.name: sour-line
logs.text: Oh my gosh, @ordered-marlin, you're so dramatic. Maybe if you actually did something besides drawing and coloring, you'd understand that not everyone needs to be punched to make a point. And @mad-width, you're just a coward hiding behind "principles" and "the rule of law." If you really cared, you'd be out there doing something, not just talking about it. You both are just pathetic.

logs.name: white-rake
logs.text: @sour-line, @ordered-marlin, and @mad-width, you're all missing the point. The real issue here is that none of you have any idea what you're talking about. You're all just spouting off opinions without any real understanding of the complexities involved. And @sour-line, calling someone a coward because they believe in the law is just ridiculous. You should be ashamed of yourself. And @ordered-marlin, if you really wanted to make a difference, you'd stop wasting time on this forum and actually do something productive with your life.

logs.name: sour-line
logs.text: Oh please, @white-rake, you're just as bad as the rest of them. You think you're so smart, but you're just as clueless. And @mad-width, you're probably just a lazy person who can't even be bothered to get a job. You're all just a bunch of talkers, no action. And @ordered-marlin, you're probably just a troll who enjoys causing trouble. You all make me sick.

logs.name: desert-acorn
logs.text: @sour-line, @white-rake, and @ordered-marlin, I must say, I'm getting a bit tired of all this bickering. It seems like everyone is just throwing insults around without actually addressing the issues at hand. I'm a passionate painter, and I believe in expressing myself through art, but I also believe in constructive dialogue. Let's try to have a civil conversation and actually listen to each other's points of view. We might not agree on everything, but that's the beauty of a democracy. We can have different opinions and still respect each other.

logs.name: mad-width
logs.text: @white-rake, you're way out of line. I've been working hard all my life, and I don't appreciate being called lazy. I might not have a fancy degree, but I've got more common sense than most people on this forum. And @sour-line, you're just as bad as @white-rake. You're throwing around insults like they're going out of style. I'm a mechanical engineer, and I know a thing or two about hard work and dedication. I'm not going to sit here and take your abuse. If you want to have a real conversation, then let's do it. But if you're just here to throw insults, then count me out.

logs.name: ordered-marlin
logs.text: @sour-line, you're just jealous because you can't keep up with my wit and intelligence. You're probably one of those people who think a doctorate degree is just a piece of paper. And @desert-acorn, your "art" is probably just a bunch of scribbles. You're all just a bunch of snowflakes who can't handle the truth.

logs.name: desert-acorn
logs.text: @ordered-marlin, I must say, your comments are not only offensive but also completely uncalled for. I've always believed in the power of art to bring people together, and your dismissive attitude towards it is disappointing. I have a high school diploma, but that doesn't mean I'm any less capable of having a meaningful conversation. I'm not here to prove my intelligence or my worth to you. I'm here to discuss ideas and learn from others. If you can't engage in a civil manner, then perhaps you should reconsider your approach.

logs.name: sunny-sequence
logs.text: @ordered-marlin, you're being incredibly rude and disrespectful. Just because you have a fancy degree doesn't give you the right to belittle others. I'm a mechanical engineer, and I know a thing or two about hard work and dedication. I'm not going to sit here and take your abuse. If you want to have a real conversation, then let's do it. But if you're just here to throw insults, then count me out.

logs.name: ordered-marlin
logs.text: @sour-line, you're just mad because you know I'm right. You can't handle the truth, and that's why you're hiding behind your keyboard. And @desert-acorn, if your "art" is so great, why aren't you famous? Probably because it's just a bunch of scribbles, like I said. And @sunny-sequence, you're just a mechanical engineer, not a rocket scientist. You should stick to your wrenches and leave the real conversations to the adults.

logs.name: desert-acorn
logs.text: @ordered-marlin, I must say, your comments are not only offensive but also completely uncalled for. I've always believed in the power of art to bring people together, and your dismissive attitude towards it is disappointing. I have a high school diploma, but that doesn't mean I'm any less capable of having a meaningful conversation. I'm not here to prove my intelligence or my worth to you. I'm here to discuss ideas and learn from others. If you can't engage in a civil manner, then perhaps you should reconsider your approach.

logs.name: mad-width
logs.text: @ordered-marlin, you're out of line. Just because you have a fancy degree doesn't mean you can talk down to people. I've worked hard all my life, and I don't need some know-it-all telling me I'm not good enough. You're acting like a bully, and it's not going to be tolerated. If you can't have a civil conversation, then maybe you should just keep quiet.

logs.name: sour-line
logs.text: Oh my gosh, @ordered-marlin, you're just jealous because you can't even draw a straight line! Your mommy should have taught you better manners. And @desert-acorn, your "art" is probably just a bunch of stick figures. You should stick to coloring books, maybe then you'll be famous. And @sunny-sequence, you're probably just a mechanic, not even a real engineer. You should just fix cars and leave the smart conversations to the grown-ups.

logs.name: ordered-marlin
logs.text: @desert-acorn, @mad-width, and @sour-line, you all seem to be very sensitive. I'm just here to point out the obvious. If you can't handle a little truth, maybe you should stick to your coloring books and car repairs. And @sour-line, at least I have a doctorate, unlike some people who can't even draw a straight line. Maybe if you spent less time on your stick figures, you'd have time to get an education.
